description: Use the test_driven optimizer to improve the complaint-generator graph analysis phase. Target files: complaint_phases/denoiser.py. Phase goal: Improve knowledge-graph and dependency-graph population so denoising and legal reasoning operate on structured facts instead of raw narrative alone. Recommended actions: Improve denoiser gap selection and answer processing so each turn closes a concrete knowledge-graph gap instead of only restating the narrative.; Make answer processing update entities, relationships, and satisfied requirements deterministically when the complainant supplies the missing field.; Promote exact dates, staff names/titles, hearing-request timing, response dates, and causation sequencing into graph updates before broadening the next question set.. Weak complaint types to generalize for: housing_discrimination, hacc_research_engine. Weak evidence modalities to improve: policy_document, file_evidence. Graph evidence targets: context_or_reason, denial_event, protected_activity. Graph focus areas: claim_elements.

metadata.workflow_phase_summary: Improve knowledge-graph and dependency-graph population so denoising and legal reasoning operate on structured facts instead of raw narrative alone.
workflow_phase_actions: Improve denoiser gap selection and answer processing so each turn closes a concrete knowledge-graph gap instead of only restating the narrative.
Make answer processing update entities, relationships, and satisfied requirements deterministically when the complainant supplies the missing field.
Promote exact dates, staff names/titles, hearing-request timing, response dates, and causation sequencing into graph updates before broadening the next question set.
cross_phase_findings: Intake questioning gaps are likely suppressing graph extraction quality; optimize question targeting before expanding graph heuristics further.
Document generation is currently bottlenecked by graph-analysis readiness; improve structured fact and requirement propagation into drafting.

graph_analysis.summary: Improve knowledge-graph and dependency-graph population so denoising and legal reasoning operate on structured facts instead of raw narrative alone.
signals: knowledge graphs average 18.00 unresolved gaps

knowledge-graph relationship growth per iteration is low
dependency satisfaction rate is only 0.11
graph-blocking intake objectives remain uncovered: causation_sequence

recommended_actions.recommended_action: Improve denoiser gap selection and answer processing so each turn closes a concrete knowledge-graph gap instead of only restating the narrative.
Make answer processing update entities, relationships, and satisfied requirements deterministically when the complainant supplies the missing field.
Promote exact dates, staff names/titles, hearing-request timing, response dates, and causation sequencing into graph updates before broadening the next question set.

document_generation.summary: Improve drafting handoff so complaint generation reflects the collected facts, exhibits, and unresolved gaps at formalization time.
signals: drafting still lacks chronology, staff-identity, or causation-sequence details needed for pleading-ready allegations
drafting depends on stronger graph and denoising handoffs
draft output is not grounded enough in canonical facts or evidence-backed support rows

recommended_actions.recommended_action: Carry blocker-closing intake answers directly into factual allegations, claim support, and exhibit descriptions so drafted complaints preserve timing, actor identity, and causation sequence.
Gate document generation on graph completeness signals and surface unresolved factual or legal gaps in drafting readiness before formalization.
Carry canonical fact ids, support traces, and artifact-backed support rows through drafting so the complaint text is grounded in traceable evidence.
